title: proboscidea parviflora subsp. parviflora
intro: Proboscidea parviflora subsp. parviflora, also known as Devil's Claw, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant's medicinal properties lie in its roots, which contain iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagoside, giving it anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of Devil's Claw include relief from arthritis, back pain, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as improved digestion and immune function. In herbal preparations, the root is typically dried and then used in teas, capsules, or tinctures, although it is also available in topical creams and ointments for localized pain relief.
